scenes.voiceover: You want your money to grow, but investing feels risky.
So you keep money in a savings account earning almost nothing.
But inflation quietly eats away at purchasing power.
Money at 0.5% interest loses value when inflation runs at 3%.
You're technically losing money by playing it safe.
In the US, there's a huge gap in financial literacy around investing.
Many don't understand 401ks, IRAs, index funds, or compound interest.
It sounds complicated, like something rich people do.
While others invest small amounts and watch it grow, you stay in place.
Decades pass, and the gap widens.
The truth is, investing doesn't have to be complicated.
Starting small with low-cost index funds is accessible.
But psychological barriers keep millions from ever starting.
Not investing is one of the biggest money mistakes, but fear makes it feel safest.
